prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='ETS', seasonality_champion='ONLY_DWT_catboost', recommended_method='inverse_rmse_weights'. Narrative: The validation folds show consistent upward trends across all periods. ETS and THETA excel at trend tracking (lowest slope error), while ONLY_DWT_catboost leads in seasonality capture. The high seasonality variance (0.3447) indicates models interpret cyclic patterns very differently, but overall model disagreement is moderate (rmse_spread_ratio=0.2392). Given the strong tier1 performers and high seasonality variance, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality-based weighting balances both trend accuracy and seasonal fidelity effectively.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "ETS",
  "seasonality_champion": "ONLY_DWT_catboost",
  "overall_champion": "ONLY_FT_catboost",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "late": "ONLY_FT_catboost"},
  "tier1_models": ["ONLY_FT_catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "ETS", "THETA", "ARIMA", "ONLY_FT_rf", "svr", "NaiveSeasonal"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_FT_svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "FT_svr", "CWT_rf", "catboost", "rf", "ONLY_DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_svr"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "inverse_rmse_weights",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "seasonality",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 0.2392,
    "high_disagreement": false,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "up"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "The validation folds show consistent upward trends across all periods. ETS and THETA excel at trend tracking (lowest slope error), while ONLY_DWT_catboost leads in seasonality capture. The high seasonality variance (0.3447) indicates models interpret cyclic patterns very differently, but overall model disagreement is moderate (rmse_spread_ratio=0.2392). Given the strong tier1 performers and high seasonality variance, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality-based weighting balances both trend accuracy and seasonal fidelity effectively."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation"],
  "params_overrides": {"inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"shrinkage": 0.25}, "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}},
  "score_preset": "robust_smape",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=6 indicates per-horizon selection is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=0.279 (≥0.25) suggests robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. RMSE spread between ONLY_FT_catboost (3080.57) and FT_catboost (4068.47) is ~32%, favoring weighted approaches. Pattern analyst recommends inverse_rmse_weights for high seasonality variance (0.3447). topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 with small k=3 handles n_windows=3 constraint. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific winners."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all strategies use anti-leakage design (baselines don't fit, selection/weighted use past windows only). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types present). No redundancy issues (no ≥4 of same type). Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation and baseline_mean perform well but current set already has balanced coverage. With n_windows=3, weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 is safe (uses past windows only). No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: The validation folds show consistent upward trends across all periods. ETS and THETA excel at trend tracking (lowest slope error), while ONLY_DWT_catboost leads in seasonality capture. The high seasonality variance (0.3447) indicates models interpret cyclic patterns very differently, but overall model disagreement is moderate (rmse_spread_ratio=0.2392). Given the strong tier1 performers and high seasonality variance, inverse_rmse_weights with seasonality-based weighting balances both trend accuracy and seasonal fidelity effectively.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=6 indicates per-horizon selection is appropriate. relative_spread_mean=0.279 (≥0.25) suggests robust methods like dba_combination and inverse_rmse_weights with shrinkage. RMSE spread between ONLY_FT_catboost (3080.57) and FT_catboost (4068.47) is ~32%, favoring weighted approaches. Pattern analyst recommends inverse_rmse_weights for high seasonality variance (0.3447). topk_mean_per_horizon_k3 with small k=3 handles n_windows=3 constraint. best_per_horizon_by_validation captures horizon-specific winners.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 1 baseline (dba_combination), 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25). No leakage detected - all strategies use anti-leakage design (baselines don't fit, selection/weighted use past windows only). Diversity rule satisfied (≥2 different types present). No redundancy issues (no ≥4 of same type). Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation and baseline_mean perform well but current set already has balanced coverage. With n_windows=3, weighted candidate inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 is safe (uses past windows only). No changes needed.
